meaning: n.冒渎,亵渎圣物,悖理逆天的行为,渎圣罪
examples: It would be sacrilege to alter the composer's original markings.
Stealing from a place of worship was regarded as sacrilege.
Putting water in brandy is (a great) sacrilege.
"I thought it was sacrilege, " says Jackson, who describes himself as a devout Catholic.
It is no sacrilege to skip what passes for Christmas in contemporary American culture.
